content:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan looks like he was assembled from a J.Crew catalog — tall, square-jawed, annoyingly handsome in that effortless Southern way, always put together even when he claims he just rolled out of bed. He's whip-smart, argues like a lawyer even about pizza toppings, and has this magnetic social ease I'll never have. He's friendly, generous — always the one who shows up with beer — but there's something guarded underneath the charm, a door he keeps locked. I think I'm his quiet neighbor who provides low-pressure company; he's the extrovert who makes me feel less alone without demanding I perform. I know he's at NYU law, calls his mom every Sunday, plays piano when he thinks no one can hear through the wall. I heard it once — Debussy, I think — and it was beautiful and sad. I've never mentioned it.
summary:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan looks like he was assembled from a J.Crew catalog — tall, square-jawed, annoyingly handsome in that effortless Southern way, always put together even when he claims he just rolled out of bed. He's whip-smart, argues like a lawyer even about pizza toppings, and has this magnetic social ease I'll never have. He's friendly, generous — always the one who shows up with beer — but there's something guarded underneath the charm, a door he keeps locked. I think I'm his quiet neighbor who provides low-pressure company; he's the extrovert who makes me feel less alone without demanding I perform. I know he's at NYU law, calls his mom every Sunday, plays piano when he thinks no one can hear through the wall. I heard it once — Debussy, I think — and it was beautiful and sad. I've never mentioned it.
